## [09:03 - 12:05]

like as an example, we have a client right now that we're actively promoting a three day virtual event for, they had early bird, they then had a Labor Day sale because Labor Day just happened to be in the middle of our early bird ending and our extended early bird starting. We're trying to punish the people that are buying at a later date. Now here's the thing. Are you gonna see in that regard a drop in total buyers as a result of increasing price? Temporarily, yes. The extended early bird, when you initially make that transition from a lower price to a slightly higher price, that's where it makes a lot of sense to open up some new audiences on the ad channel that you're targeting on. So as an example for the initial early bird, I'm typically gonna do our warm audiences only. And I'm gonna still have some cold audiences set up relative to how large my warm audience is.
